concerns.text: FR-004 specifies computing a Spearman rank correlation between each taxon’s CLR‑transformed abundance trajectory and the individual’s UPDRS Part III score change per month. This treats longitudinal observations as independent points, ignoring within‑patient repeated measures. In longitudinal microbiome studies, such an approach can inflate type‑I error because the correlation does not account for the nested data structure. A more statistically sound method would be to model the association directly with a mixed‑effects model (as already required in FR-005) or to aggregate trajectories appropriately before correlation. The current design risks invalid inference.
